tweets.text: Jimmy Carter established his reputation as an international peacemaker largely through his mediation of the 1978 Camp David Accords, which were signed by Egyptian President Sadat and Israeli Prime Minister Begin at the White House in September 1978. More: https://t.co/vedKIECnP8

Human rights are not the heritage or the aspiration of any one nationality, any one faith, any one ideology, any one region, or any other characteristic – they're universal. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/irRtIxpu0u

Putin’s unjust invasion of Ukraine is an imperial project to erase Ukraine from the map. 

Every country has a sovereign right to make their own choices — including the Ukrainian people’s right to choose their own future, not have their choices made in and by Moscow. https://t.co/wUM00Ll9AE

We know the challenges that define our age demand African leadership. One out of every four human beings on Earth will live in Africa by the year 2050. The ingenuity and determination of young Africans in particular … will be undeniable forces in that human progress. –@POTUS https://t.co/LdCUytqEe8

We have a responsibility — a responsibility to stop the suffering, to end this war, to support the Sudanese people so that freedom is once again on their faces. Last week, @SecBlinken announced the U.S. is providing nearly $200M in additional humanitarian assistance. https://t.co/F6xqQi7JK4

In the aftermath, the United States undertook a massive humanitarian response and contributed over $400 million to relief efforts. The American people contributed more than $700 million through private-sector donations. https://t.co/JuTznwJnRu

Twenty years later, we remember the devastating Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami that took more than 240,000 lives. We honor the victims and stand in solidarity and friendship with all nations and people impacted by the disaster. https://t.co/xZJPCu9EXU
Wishing peace and prosperity to all communities celebrating the start of Kwanzaa. https://t.co/oX9mbPABlV
Chag sameach to all who are marking the beginning of Hanukkah this evening. 🕎 https://t.co/IzH5yWAKUV

Each winter, Americans of diverse backgrounds celebrate a variety of religious and cultural holidays with neighbors, family, and friends. @ShareAmerica shares photos of the joyful traditions — from Christmas and Hanukkah to winter solstice celebrations. https://t.co/ALnaeWH1Fl

The State Department wishes our staff, friends, allies, partners, and all who are celebrating a very Merry Christmas. May your holiday gatherings with friends and family be joyous and festive. 🎄 https://t.co/frgTpEX41M

Whether by accident or inspiration, certain towns across the U.S. — from North Pole, Alaska, to Christmas Cove, Maine, to Garland, Texas — get extra attention each December and in some ways embody the spirit of the holiday season. @ShareAmerica explains: https://t.co/jRHpwnNeKI

The United States has been mobilizing a global response to address both the perils and opportunities of artificial intelligence. Together with our partners, we’ve developed a global consensus for AI, and now we’re building upon it. https://t.co/Upgg1uqFl5

In @StateDeputySpox Patel's final Week at State, @SecBlinken continues travel to Iraq, then to Jordan, discussing support for the Syrian people with regional partners. Later, in New York, he participates in @UN Security Council sessions on Sudan and artificial intelligence. https://t.co/2azPkR6G1F

Additionally, we are mobilizing over $100 million in public and private resources with leading AI developers to support computer access, skills training, and datasets for partners in developing countries. https://t.co/0IViokpDHo
The United States and 58 other partners have endorsed rules of the road for the responsible use of AI and autonomy in the military domain. https://t.co/ngr0PobhZQ

The United States forged global consensus on the critical importance of safe, secure, and trustworthy AI for sustainable development through the first-ever @UN General Assembly resolution on AI, co-sponsored by 122 other countries and adopted by consensus. https://t.co/v19UHPV2T8

The United States and our @G7 allies developed the first-ever multilateral framework for developers of advanced AI systems, called the Hiroshima AI Process Comprehensive Policy Framework. https://t.co/DDfbO6yxid

At the dawn of the AI revolution, the United States led the way in establishing the rules of the road. We’ve demonstrated how AI can be safe, secure, and trustworthy, while also unlocking opportunities to solve the world’s greatest challenges. https://t.co/s23f2IyVIT
.@SecBlinken recently chaired a @UN Security Council meeting on the impact artificial intelligence could have on international peace and security. Here's what you need to know.🧵

In his conversation with @CFR_org's Michael Froman, @SecBlinken talked about how this administration has built bridges across Europe and the Indo-Pacific that have partners working together in new ways. Because what happens in one place has profound consequences in the other. https://t.co/czPdtYOdrZ

We’re entering a new phase of foreign affairs, with intense competition shaping what comes next. The U.S. is strong, thanks to historic investments at home. Secretary Blinken explains to @ForeignAffairs how we restored our competitiveness. https://t.co/6peQeqloP4 https://t.co/Qw0nOYxkmx
https://t.co/2ldVRJrn7F
.@SecBlinken participates in a @UN Security Council Session on Sudan in New York City. https://t.co/vDxPgPHkc7  https://t.co/eYhBjQ0bDw
.@SecBlinken participates in a @UN Security Council Session on Artificial Intelligence in New York City.  https://t.co/z3WtmYKNsL https://t.co/w5TcN5RQze
.@SecBlinken participates in a conversation at the Council on Foreign Relations in New York City. https://t.co/9QDQ16RCsu https://t.co/iQQ4Ju6oV9

There is no military solution. Now is the time to catalyze broader international action to prevent atrocities.

Sudan’s people need safe access to humanitarian assistance, including by enabling humanitarian corridors to allow the delivery of aid and movement of civilians. https://t.co/3h8ZVKf1GZ

Despite heroic efforts by humanitarian workers, vital aid delivery has been obstructed by both the warring parties.

The people of Sudan desperately need an immediate end to the fighting. https://t.co/4XnUwwR5Sq

After 20 months of fighting that has devastated agricultural production, destroyed market systems, displaced 12 million people, and obstructed humanitarian operations, Sudan is now facing the worst levels of acute food insecurity ever recorded in the country. https://t.co/cJU3htUd9Y

Conflict broke out between Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) on April 15, 2023.

UN Security Council resolutions on Sudan have since called for a cessation of hostilities and for full, rapid, safe, and unhindered humanitarian access. https://t.co/LQab3qr2H4

Sudan’s civil war has brought about the world’s largest humanitarian crisis—over 30 million people are in desperate need of emergency assistance.

As @SecBlinken joins @UN Security Council debate on Sudan this week, here's what you need to know. 🧵
Our message to the Syrian people is this: We want them to succeed and we are prepared to help them do so. https://t.co/t1jI2qqqoB

During @SecBlinken’s recent trip to the Middle East, the U.S. and partners in the region agreed on the need for a unified approach in helping the Syrian people emerge from a past dominated by the Assad regime.
https://t.co/QXF2uiMqsK

Ukrainian innovation is transforming lives and safeguarding Europe’s future. From AI-powered prosthetics to drone technology to agricultural exports, solidarity with Ukraine is leading to innovation, empowerment, and progress. https://t.co/Mk1Lmj42RD

This week at State, @SecBlinken recognizes Global Anti-Corruption Champions and Human Rights Defenders, former Secretary @JohnKerry’s official portrait gets unveiled, and the Secretary travels to Jordan and Türkiye, discussing the latest developments in Syria with leaders. https://t.co/a7LiPlwzmd
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Jordan. https://t.co/A2RMmd42Ov  https://t.co/DahDmv9yZT

.@SecBlinken spoke to the press before departing Baghdad. He reiterated that the U.S. is determined to work with Iraq on its security and its sovereignty amid the impact that the events in Syria will have. He emphasized that it’s critical that we all support the Syrian people as they emerge from years of the Assad regime’s dictatorship.

@SecBlinken The Secretary then flew to Baghdad to meet with Iraqi Prime Minister Mohammed Shiaa al-Sudani. The U.S. is committed to our strategic partnership with Iraq and to its security, stability, and sovereignty. https://t.co/hh1bBS42Rl

The day began in Ankara, where the Secretary met with Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan to discuss the latest on Syria. @SecBlinken reaffirmed U.S. support for a Syrian-led and Syrian-owned political transition to an accountable and inclusive government. https://t.co/oMzbnlg22J

.@SecBlinken wrapped the second day of his trip, taking him from Türkiye to Iraq.

Here’s a recap. ⤵️ https://t.co/kDxRhv4H3S
The United States supports an inclusive, Syrian-led transition to an accountable and representative government. https://t.co/Davdzt5cQr

The Syrian people will decide the future of Syria. The United States reaffirms its full support for a Syrian-owned political transition, which must include clear commitments to fully respect the rights of vulnerable communities and prevent Syria from being a base for terrorism. https://t.co/5RGaHk004Y

Our @NATO Ally Türkiye is a key partner in developing a peaceful future for Syria.

During a meeting in Ankara, @SecBlinken and President Erdogan discussed the latest developments in Syria and U.S.-Turkish regional cooperation. https://t.co/2dwO4UxAiO

@KingAbdullahII @AymanHsafadi Before he departed Jordan, @SecBlinken told the press the focus of his trip is to help coordinate efforts across the region to support the Syrian people as they transition away from Assad’s brutal dictatorship. https://t.co/f01nYiB1en

The U.S. is also committed to supporting the stability of Syria’s neighbors, including Jordan, during this period of transition.

In Aqaba, the Secretary met with Jordanian @KingAbdullahII and Foreign Minister @AymanHsafadi to discuss developments in Syria and our efforts to secure peace in the region.

Today @SecBlinken traveled to Jordan and Türkiye to discuss the situation in Syria. The United States, working alongside partners, will help support  an inclusive, Syrian-led transition to an accountable and representative government. 🧵 https://t.co/DAxTCCzZQ1

.@RFJ_USA is offering a $10M reward for information on missing American Austin Tice. We are committed to bringing Austin home. If anyone has information leading to his location, recovery, and return, you can send a message via Signal, WhatsApp, or Telegram to 1-202-702-7843.

The signing highlights the growth of U.S. space diplomacy through the Artemis Accords and emphasizes the importance of global space cooperation. Together, we’re building the broadest and most diverse coalition ever. https://t.co/uqOww5ss5B

What does the State Department have to do with space exploration? Plenty! We work with our colleagues at NASA to bring together nations with a common vision for peaceful, sustainable, and transparent cooperation in space. https://t.co/yXSb5FDe05

This week we welcomed Austria as the 50th signatory to the Artemis Accords during an event at @NASA headquarters. This milestone strengthens the international commitment to peaceful space exploration and use. #ArtemisAccords #SpaceDiplomacy https://t.co/iHuYvchux8

Anti-corruption champions shine a light on truth and accountability. Today, we honor those who work for transparency, justice, and integrity worldwide. To hear more about our other champions, watch the full ceremony here: https://t.co/txABUYSsyM

Champion Lt. Col. Jorge Mora Cortes of Colombia is the head of the Anti-Corruption Investigation Office of the Colombian National Police and worked hard to dismantle the largest trade-based money laundering ring in Columbia. Due to his work, he was forced to relocate his family. https://t.co/4dvVbKTmIo

Sevinj Vagifgizi of Azerbaijan is currently being unjustly detained for writing news stories concerning illegal tenders to companies controlled by government officials and investigating officials in order to expose public funds embezzlement. https://t.co/RMJZMMKgRd

Dr. Srbuhi Galyan has helped Armenia use asset forfeiture as an effective anti-corruption tool, with 2024 anti-corruption cases leading to over $1.5 billion in forfeitures and eight settlement agreements returning over $12 million to Armenia. https://t.co/Vn6Tr0yvkc

This week @SecBlinken recognized 10 champions who have demonstrated leadership, courage, and impact in preventing, exposing, and combating corruption. We reaffirm our commitment to all those working to build transparency and accountability around the world. https://t.co/yTXWHxt5RD

Human rights are not the heritage or the aspiration of any one nationality, any one faith, any one ideology, any one region, or any other characteristic – they're universal. — @SecBlinken at the Human Rights Defender Award Ceremony https://t.co/ig4kjW5yzc
.@SecBlinken testifies before the House Foreign Affairs Committee on the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan on Capitol Hill. https://t.co/kcOB6SfgeK  https://t.co/VnHQS8M4p1

Today @SecBlinken recognized eight human rights defenders who have promoted respect for human rights, advocated for government transparency, and fought corruption. Learn more about the 2024 Human Rights Defenders Award recipients: https://t.co/KcqTpCVNyW https://t.co/I4btHW4v5r
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the Human Rights Defender Award Ceremony. https://t.co/GBpnyFUkFA https://t.co/yjAp8SnqrV

During former Secretary Kerry’s tenure as America’s top diplomat from 2013 to 2017, he guided the Department’s strategy, including the successful negotiation of the Iran nuclear deal and the Paris Climate Agreement. https://t.co/Hgo7AbF0au

This week, former Secretary of State @JohnKerry’s portrait was unveiled. It will join the official portraits of all former secretaries of state. Secretary Kerry served as the 68th Secretary of State under the Obama Administration. https://t.co/09rXCLljmF

We will support international efforts to hold the Assad regime and its backers accountable for atrocities and abuses perpetrated against the Syrian people, including the use of chemical weapons and the unjust detention of civilians such as Austin Tice.
https://t.co/BMIOA8fDFN

The U.S. strongly supports a peaceful transition of power to an accountable Syrian government through an inclusive Syrian-led process.

The region and the world have a responsibility to support the Syrian people as they begin to rebuild their country and chart a new direction.

After 14 years of conflict and decades of brutal repression under the Assad regime, Syrians finally feel hope for their future.

The end of this regime is a defeat for all who enabled its barbarity and its corruption — none more than Iran, Hezbollah, and Russia. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/2rkvQYx8Zo
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at a portrait unveiling of the 68th Secretary of State @JohnKerry at the State Department. https://t.co/fFeWDbncqV  https://t.co/hqcQkDuK1u
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the U.S International Development Finance Corporation @DFCgov 5th Anniversary Conference, in Washington, D.C. https://t.co/OPmmtP5VqV https://t.co/KozJn2Tvqk
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the Anti-Corruption Champions Awards Ceremony at the State Department. https://t.co/lyNH8JwAN8 https://t.co/QXVMWFsD4V

Over 150 @TechWomen from 21 countries and 45 U.S. mentors came together in Morocco last week for TechWomen’s first-ever Global Alumnae Summit to connect, empower, and inspire a global network of women leaders and drive equity in #STEM worldwide. https://t.co/8Yy8NuD1kz

Putin’s unjust invasion of Ukraine is an imperial project to erase Ukraine from the map.

Every country has a sovereign right to make their own choices — including the Ukrainian people’s right to choose their own future, not have their choices made in and by Moscow. https://t.co/hblg9XGrMB

This week at State, @POTUS travels to Angola to spotlight how U.S. infrastructure investments are connecting southern Africa to the global economy. And @SecBlinken travels to Belgium for meetings with @NATO Foreign Ministers, followed by Malta for @OSCE meetings. https://t.co/4BElQ2evcu
Putin time and again has sought to break Allied resolve as well as the resolve of the Ukrainian people. It hasn’t happened. It won’t happen. https://t.co/xjwYuRPmGL

The United States is expanding our relationship all across Africa — from assistance to aid to investment to trade — moving from patrons to partners to help bridge the infrastructure gap. – @POTUS https://t.co/u7f6bib02S

We know the challenges that define our age demand African leadership. One out of every four human beings on Earth will live in Africa by the year 2050. And the ingenuity and determination of young Africans in particular … will be undeniable forces in that human progress. – @POTUS
We seek a better way: transparent, high-standard, open-access investments that protect workers and the rule of law and the environment. – @POTUS on U.S. investment in Africa https://t.co/9BqK8uVMlg

Learn more about how the U.S. partnership with Angola is bringing major investments in transportation, clean energy, and other projects that will spur economic growth for both nations. https://t.co/LZYles6gU9
Upgraded logistics networks will make it easier to move items like seeds and fertilizers to farms and finished products to markets. https://t.co/mWB95ftAIm
Using the Corridor will reduce the time goods spend in transit, minimizing spoilage, and boost farmers’ incomes by ensuring more fresh produce reaches the market. https://t.co/Q5vkjYtlkh

The Lobito Trans-Africa Corridor will attract investments in agribusiness, including processing facilities and storage units, boosting the value chain for agricultural products. https://t.co/xiBzQhyKs4
By linking the Atlantic and Indian oceans, the Lobito Trans-Africa Corridor will open new export routes, giving farmers access to new markets. https://t.co/2YeByKH9uZ
A more direct route to ports and markets reduces transportation times and helps farmers get their produce to customers faster. https://t.co/4YWn3Q4kFs

Through the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment, the U.S. is helping expand the Lobito Trans-Africa Corridor. This transformative economic corridor, anchored in core rail investments, is spurring economic growth and revitalized trade routes for the region. https://t.co/nMMi7RO0Ve

In recognition of his significant efforts in support of Sweden’s accession to NATO, Swedish Foreign Minister Stenergard presented @SecBlinken with Sweden’s Order of the Polar Star. Transatlantic security is stronger with Sweden’s accession. https://t.co/yaLVaWa9x6

Finally, in a meeting with civil society members from Ukraine, Azerbaijan, Belarus, and Russia, @SecBlinken praised the role of civil society in holding governments accountable and preserving, defending, and protecting basic human freedoms. https://t.co/hPVjXrF6UZ

At #OSCEMC24, the Secretary’s remarks noted that @OSCE embodies the highest standards of democracy, human rights, the rule of law and, despite Russia’s attempts to obstruct their work, the OSCE continues to carry out its mission. https://t.co/tddwnqyZEh

@SecBlinken While on travel, the Secretary often stops to thank the staff and families that breathe life into our embassies and consulates. At @usembmalta, @SecBlinken recognized the work they do to strengthen U.S.-Malta ties and deepen friendships between our peoples. https://t.co/ei4rAR9z5I

With Malta as the host of this year’s OSCE Ministerial, @SecBlinken began his trip with a meeting with Malta’s leadership. The Secretary and Maltese Prime Minister Abela reaffirmed the partnership and friendship between the United States and Malta. https://t.co/1EbM0eeM62

After diplomatic engagements with @NATO Allies in Brussels this week, @SecBlinken was off to Malta to attend the @OSCE Ministerial Council meeting.

Here’s a recap. https://t.co/b9O8yzyQYn

In the past four years, the @NATO Alliance has undertaken the most significant reforms and the most significant strengthening that we’ve seen in decades. 

NATO is bigger, it’s stronger, it’s better resourced to meet the challenges ahead. https://t.co/DlwVmFF6EV

A stronger @NATO means more capabilities to deter aggression, more effective Allies to meet more complex challenges, and the peace and stability that allows our people to pursue fuller lives. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/qSFZbsfN5f

When we all talk about this powerful desire to avoid conflicts, to prevent wars, to have peace, the best way to do that is through ongoing investment in the world’s most successful defensive Alliance, and that is NATO. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/7yMI2HgIuc

The U.S. and our @G7 partners want people everywhere to thrive. That means helping friends in emerging markets develop what they need to participate in the global economy. And its why we launched the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment. https://t.co/xlR5iRk9M0

For 75 years, @‌NATO has served as a shield against aggression, facilitating governments and society in achieving a fuller and happier life for all our citizens. Learn how NATO’s contributions have contributed to our daily lives. https://t.co/kVlBy9DYoY

At the heart of NATO is a commitment from each Ally to come to the defense of any other Ally. That means that a would-be aggressor knows that if they pick a fight with one, they pick a fight with all. https://t.co/x9UC4gNAE2
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability from Brussels, Belgium. https://t.co/m8ExXGvbfP https://t.co/mNbsb95kyu

The U.S. partnership with Angola is bringing major investments in transportation, clean energy, and other projects that will spur economic growth for both nations. Learn more from @ShareAmerica:
https://t.co/LZYles6OJH

Disability rights is foreign policy. Follow along for a day in the life with #SpecialAdvisorDisability Minkara as she talks about her work to ensure equal access and opportunity for individuals with disabilities. https://t.co/cccX0wd2e8
.@DeputySecState Campbell hosts a preview of the USA Pavilion at Expo 2025 Osaka, at the State Department. https://t.co/keMZhPgT8x

.@SecBlinken: "When we incorporate the needs, the insights of people with disabilities, we all benefit from their talents, from their expertise, from their leadership.” Hear his remarks on our commitment to disability inclusive efforts: https://t.co/rfYlv9ewRx

Under the leadership of @SecBlinken and Special Advisor on International Disability Rights Sara Minkara, we’ve worked with our key global partners over the last four years to advance disability inclusion, access, and opportunity.

Everyone benefits when people with disabilities can fully participate in their communities. Learn how the Department has helped build a more equitable, accessible, and inclusive world: https://t.co/LvGCEHq2uM
